records.ety: [OE. suffisen, OF. soufire, F. suffire (cf. suffisant, p. pr.), L. sufficere to put under, to substitute, to avail for, to suffice; sub under + facere to make. See Fact.]
records.morph: [imp. & p. p. Sufficed (?); p. pr. & vb. n. Sufficing (?).]

defs: To be enough, or sufficient; to meet the need (of anything); to be equal to the end proposed; to be adequate.
quotes: To recount almighty works, What words or tongue of seraph can suffice?

defs: To satisfy; to content; to be equal to the wants or demands of.
quotes: Let it suffice thee; speak no more unto me of this matter.
The power appeased, with winds sufficed the sail.
